markets.question: Map Handicap: PR (-1.5) vs DRX (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Valorant Lower bracket quarterfinal 2 match between Paper Rex and DRX in the VCT Pacific Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 10 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Paper Rex" if Paper Rex wins 2 or more maps than DRX in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "DRX". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Valorant: Paper Rex vs DRX (BO3) - VCT Pacific Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Valorant Lower bracket quarterfinal 2 match between Paper Rex and DRX in the VCT Pacific Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 10 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Paper Rex" if Paper Rex win the match against DRX.

This market will resolve to "DRX" if DRX win the match against Paper Rex.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paper Rex enters the VCT Pacific Stage 1 playoffs lower bracket quarterfinals as the perceived favorite against Kiwoom DRX in this Bo3, following a 1-2 upset loss to Global Esports in upper bracket semifinals on May 8 that dropped the Group Alpha #1 seed. DRX, the #2 seed from the same group, advanced via a 2-1 lower round 1 win over Nongshim RedForce yesterday after an opening 0-2 defeat to FULL SENSE. PRX dominated their recent group stage matchup 2-0 (Breeze 13-5, Lotus 13-5 on May 3), extending a 2-1 Kickoff win, with head-to-head favoring them 6-4 over the last 10 series. PRX's proactive map control and untraded kill edges (10.4% first kills vs. 6.4% first deaths) suit the current low-util meta, while both teams' 4-1 group records (+48 and +17 map differentials) signal a competitive clash for Masters qualification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election winner at 18.8%, closely trailed by California Governor Gavin Newsom (16.7%) and Secretary of State Marco Rubio (15.4%), reflecting the fluid dynamics of an early primary cycle still over two years out. Vance's lead stems from his incumbency advantage and topping the March CPAC straw poll at 53%, though recent polls show slippage amid favorability concerns. Rubio has surged on viral White House remarks showcasing broad appeal and strong GOP electability ratings, while Newsom builds Democratic momentum through fundraisers with fellow governors and leads in early California primary surveys. The 2026 midterms loom as a pivotal test for party control and candidate viability, with fundraising, endorsements, and national profiles poised to create separation in this contested field.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold (GC) futures for June 2026 trade near $4,724 per ounce, reflecting trader consensus on sustained upside from central bank purchases—nearing record levels—and expectations of Federal Reserve rate cuts amid cooling but sticky inflation. Recent April 2026 FOMC signals of policy easing, coupled with a softer U.S. dollar and 10-year Treasury yields dipping below 4%, have bolstered safe-haven demand amid geopolitical risks. Key drivers include robust ETF inflows and emerging market reserve diversification. Traders eye May CPI data (due mid-June) and the June 10-11 FOMC meeting as pivotal catalysts that could push prices toward $5,000 if disinflation accelerates, or trigger pullbacks on hotter-than-expected readings.

markets.question: FC Barcelona vs. Real Madrid CF: O/U 9.5 Total Corners
markets.description: In the upcoming La Liga game between FC Barcelona and Real Madrid CF, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Over" if there are 10 or more total corners awarded in this game (combining corners for both teams). If the total number of corners is fewer than 10, this market will resolve to "Under". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. This market refers only to corners awarded within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Corners awarded during extra time or penalty shootouts do not count. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Barcelona vs. Real Madrid CF - Total Corners
events.description: Total corners markets for the La Liga game between FC Barcelona and Real Madrid CF, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona top the La Liga table with a commanding lead over second-placed Real Madrid ahead of their pivotal El Clásico on May 10 at Spotify Camp Nou, where high-stakes title implications fuel aggressive play. Both sides generate substantial corners—Barcelona averaging 7.35 at home with matches totaling 11.27, Real Madrid 6.13 away amid 10.18 overall—reflecting possession-dominant styles and frequent wide attacks under Hansi Flick and Carlo Ancelotti. Recent developments include Kylian Mbappé's hamstring injury rendering him doubtful, compounded by Rodrygo's season-ending ACL tear, potentially limiting Madrid's crossing volume, while Raphinha eases back from his own hamstring issue for Barça. Past Clásicos average over 11 corners, underscoring rivalry-driven intensity.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on peak 2026 inflation reflects a sharp rebound in recent data, with March CPI rising to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—and PCE inflation hitting 3.5%, driven by surging energy costs and passthrough from new trade tariffs. Core measures also accelerated, with core PCE at 3.2%, prompting the Federal Reserve's March dot plot to lift end-2026 PCE projections to 2.7% from prior estimates. Markets price heightened uncertainty amid sticky services inflation and potential tariff escalation, contrasting analyst forecasts ranging from 2.8% to over 4%. Key catalysts ahead include tomorrow's April CPI release on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, where policy response could recalibrate rate cut expectations.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Beijing be 32°C on May 11?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Beijing Capital International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/beijing/ZBAA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight clustering in global forecast models like ECMWF and GFS, with ensemble means projecting Beijing's May 11 high temperature around 31°C amid North China's first heatwave of 2026, where high pressure ridges promote subsidence, clear skies, and above-seasonal averages (historical May highs near 27°C). The China Meteorological Administration's latest outlook aligns near 30°C, but model member spread—driven by uncertainties in afternoon cloud cover, wind shear, and peak solar heating—differentiates 31°C (34% implied probability) from 32°C (30%), with lower odds for extremes. Key resolution hinges on official daily maximum at Beijing Capital International Airport; watch 12z model runs and real-time observations today for shifts before tomorrow's peak.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol remains in detention following his February 2026 life imprisonment sentence for leading an insurrection tied to his 2024 martial law declaration, after the Constitutional Court upheld his impeachment and removal from office in April 2025. Multiple convictions, including a five-year term for obstructing arrest (upheld and increased to seven years by an appeals court on April 29, 2026), reinforce trader consensus implying a 92.1% probability he stays in custody through 2026. Ongoing appeals in his insurrection trial, with hearings as recent as May 7, show no bail grants or release signals, amid procedural hurdles like extended detention warrants that historically prolong high-profile cases. Late-breaking acquittals or health-related pardons could shift odds, though none appear imminent.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil June 2026 futures settled at $94.68, down 0.74 amid a recent 4% pullback as Middle East geopolitical risks eased following U.S. military de-escalation signals and Gulf stabilization proposals. This retreat from May highs near $106 reflects trader consensus on peaking supply disruptions, with EIA's Short-Term Energy Outlook forecasting Brent at $115 per barrel in Q2 before declining on non-OPEC+ output gains and softening global demand growth from China. Backwardation in the curve signals near-term tightness, but persistent U.S. inventory builds pose downside risks. Traders eye weekly EIA reports, OPEC+ monitoring on May 13, and June 30 settlement for volatility.

markets.question: Will Arsenal score the most goals in 2025-26 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the club that records the most total goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Champions League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the club who advanced farther in the competition. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the club that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Champions League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/statistics/clubs/goals/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain holds a commanding 44 goals in 16 UEFA Champions League matches, recently overtaking Bayern Munich's 43 after a record-breaking semi-final tie totaling 11 goals across legs (PSG advancing 6-5 aggregate). With Bayern eliminated, Arsenal trails at 29 goals entering the final despite strong knockout form, including clean sheets in prior rounds. Trader consensus prices PSG at 99.4% implied probability to claim top-scoring club honors, reflecting the insurmountable 15-goal deficit—Arsenal would require scoring 16+ more than PSG in one match, an unprecedented outcome. PSG's distributed attack, led by Kvaratskhelia (10 goals), bolsters their position amid peaking momentum.

Over the past 30 days, Shimizu notched a convincing 3-0 clean-sheet victory over V-Varen Nagasaki on April 5 before drawing 1-1 at Sanfrecce Hiroshima on April 11, positioning them mid-table around 9th-12th. Avispa, lower in the standings near 10th-23rd, earned a vital 1-0 away win over V-Varen on April 11 to halt a winless streak, but face challenges away from Best Denki Stadium. No major injuries reported for either side, though lineup confirmations and weather could influence over/under goals props.

markets.question: Will Anthropic be acquired before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if credible reporting confirms that any entity enters into an agreement to acquire the listed company by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat April McClain Delaney leads trader consensus at 87.5% implied probability to win Maryland's 6th Congressional District House seat, reflecting the district's Safe Democratic rating from Sabato's Crystal Ball and Cook Political Report amid a partisan lean favoring Democrats by wide margins in recent cycles. A Public Sentiment Institute poll from April 8-11 showed Delaney at 55% against a generic Republican at 31%, with challenger David Trone polling even stronger at 59%, underscoring Democratic dominance regardless of the June 23 primary outcome between the heavily self-funded rivals. Weak Republican fundraising—top GOP candidates under $310,000 raised—and historical incumbency advantages in this suburban-rural district sustain low odds for Republicans at 9%, though national midterm dynamics could introduce volatility ahead of November 3.

markets.question: Will "Mortal Kombat II" Opening Weekend Box Office be between 45m and 50m?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to how much "Mortal Kombat II" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (May 8 - May 10) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

If there is no final data available by May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Mortal Kombat II** tracking has converged on a $35-40 million domestic opening as the trader-favored outcome at 74.5% implied probability, driven by Thursday previews grossing $5.2 million—more than double the 2021 original's—and a solid $17 million Friday from 3,500 theaters, signaling strong fan turnout for Karl Urban's Johnny Cage debut and franchise Easter eggs. A franchise-record 90% Rotten Tomatoes audience score fuels word-of-mouth momentum, complemented by a fresh 70% critics' rating, though story critiques temper broader appeal. Competition from **The Devil Wears Prada 2**'s robust second-weekend hold around $40 million, plus Mother's Day family skews, caps projections below initial $50 million estimates; final tallies post-Sunday will resolve amid high volatility in action sequels.

markets.question: Will Trump’s approval rating be at least 40.0 on May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to Silver Bulletin's approval rating for Donald Trump on May 15, 2026.

Note that the approval ratings for this date must be finalized before it is considered for this market (namely, once the next data point is available, the previous one is finalized).

This market's resolution source will be Silver Bulletin's approval rating poll aggregator, https://www.natesilver.net/p/trump-approval-ratings-nate-silver-bulletin, specifically the approval rating indicated by the green trend line for the resolution date. Changes in the methodology by which Silver Bulletin calculates the approval rating will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. If Silver Bulletin's approval rating becomes permanently unavailable, RealClearPolitics will be used.

The resolution source reports the rating value to only one decimal point (e.g., 42.8%, 33.9%, etc.). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve has held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% for three consecutive meetings, most recently on April 29, 2026, amid March CPI inflation rising to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—fueled by a 21.2% gasoline price surge. April nonfarm payrolls added a modest 115,000 jobs, with unemployment unchanged at 4.3%, signaling labor market stability that tempers rate-cut urgency. The March 2026 dot plot median projects gradual easing to the low-3% range by end-2027, shaping trader consensus on Polymarket for limited downside before then. Traders eye April CPI on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC for shifts in monetary policy expectations, with Treasury yields and futures curves reflecting potential upside risks from persistent inflation.

markets.question: Will Meituan have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Telstar 1963 vs. Heracles Almelo: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game between Telstar 1963 and Heracles Almelo, scheduled for May 10 at 10:45 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Telstar 1963 and Heracles Almelo combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Telstar 1963 vs. Heracles Almelo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for May 10 at 10:45 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Telstar host Heracles Almelo in a pivotal Eredivisie relegation showdown at BUKO Stadion on May 10, with both clubs mired near the table bottom and desperate for points to avoid direct drop or playoffs. Telstar remain winless in their last six league games, struggling for goals despite modest home record of three wins, while Heracles endure a nine-match winless streak including seven straight away losses, exposing defensive frailties. Their season encounter ended 1-1, preserving Heracles' unbeaten streak across eight head-to-heads. Injuries sideline Telstar's Nökkvi Thorisson and Dion Malone alongside Heracles' Mike Te Wierik and Jeff Reine-Adélaide, heightening uncertainty in this low-scoring, survival clash.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

events.title: NY-08 House Election Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Hakeem Jeffries' commanding position as House Minority Leader in the solidly Democratic NY-08 district, rated Solid D by Cook Political Report with a D+26 partisan lean, drives trader consensus heavily favoring the Democratic Party at 92% for the November 3 general election. Jeffries dominates the June 23 Democratic primary market at 96% against challenger Vance Bostic, reflecting strong fundraising and establishment support amid minimal recent primary turbulence. No prominent Republican nominee has emerged, underscoring historical lopsided margins exceeding 70%. Scenarios like a primary upset yielding a flawed nominee, Jeffries scandal, or national Republican wave could shift odds, though structural advantages make an upset unlikely.

markets.question: Will Estonia be in the top 3 at Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 3 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

events.title: Eurovision 2026: Top 3
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 3 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's Linda Lampenius & Pete Parkkonen lead trader consensus across Polymarket and bookmakers for Eurovision 2026 top 3 placement with "Liekinheitin," reflecting strong staging in second rehearsals and broad jury-televote appeal that propelled them past early challengers like Denmark. Greece's Akylas ("Ferto") and Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund ("Før vi går hjem") hold firm in the mix, boosted by pre-party momentum, while Romania's Alexandra Căpitănescu ("Choke Me") surges on vocal prowess and visuals from recent clips, and Israel's Noam Bettan ("Michelle") dominates televote odds. With 35 entries confirmed for Vienna's Wiener Stadthalle, semifinals on May 12 and 14 will confirm qualifiers before the May 16 final, where last-minute jury splits or diaspora voting could spark upsets.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Morgan Stanley commands a 46% implied probability as the lead underwriter for SpaceX's anticipated blockbuster IPO—potentially a $75 billion raise at $1.75 trillion valuation—owing to its longstanding advisory ties to Elon Musk, including key roles in Tesla's 2010 public offering and recent tender offers. Financial Times and Reuters reporting since late 2025 highlights Morgan Stanley banker Michael Grimes spearheading "Project Apex," the internal IPO codename, edging out Goldman Sachs (17.5%) and Bank of America (15%), both tapped for senior roles alongside JPMorgan and up to 21 banks total. Trader sentiment reflects this hierarchy amid preparations for an S-1 filing by mid-May 2026, June roadshow, and listing, though final roles remain unconfirmed amid high oversubscription risks.

markets.question: Nvidia Data Center Revenue above 50B in Q1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Nvidia’s data center revenue for the first fiscal quarter of 2027, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.
The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released, and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 
 
If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market. 

The resolution source for this market is Nvidia’s official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects strong confidence in NVIDIA's data center revenue exceeding key thresholds for fiscal Q1 2027 (ending April 2026), driven by the segment's explosive 75% year-over-year growth to a record $62.3 billion in Q4 FY2026, which accounted for 91% of total $68.1 billion revenue. Management's $78 billion (±2%) total revenue guidance—explicitly excluding China data center compute amid U.S. export curbs—implies sequential acceleration, with analysts projecting data center sales around $70 billion fueled by Blackwell GPU ramp-up and hyperscaler AI capex surges. Key watchpoints include gross margins (guided 74.9%) and Q2 outlook; earnings release on May 20 could trigger volatility based on guidance beats versus Street estimates.

markets.question: Valorant: Sentinels vs NRG - Map 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Sentinels and NRG in the VCT Americas Stage 1 Group Omega, initially scheduled for May 10 at 8:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Sentinels" if Sentinels win Map 1 against NRG.

This market will resolve to "NRG" if NRG win Map 1 against Sentinels.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 1. 

If Map 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Valorant: Sentinels vs NRG (BO3) - VCT Americas Stage 1 Group Omega
events.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Sentinels and NRG in the VCT Americas Stage 1 Group Omega, initially scheduled for May 10 at 8:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Sentinels" if Sentinels win the match against NRG.

This market will resolve to "NRG" if NRG win the match against Sentinels.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Group Omega of VCT Americas Stage 1, Sentinels and NRG sit at 2-2 records, with top-four advancement to playoffs on the line in this decisive Bo3 on May 10. Sentinels, featuring new duelist Jerrwin, rebounded from an 0-2 opener loss to KRÜ Esports with 2-0 sweeps over 100 Thieves and Evil Geniuses, but stumbled 1-2 to FURIA on May 2 amid close maps on Split, Breeze, and Haven. NRG mirrored the pattern, dropping 0-2 decisions to FURIA and KRÜ before grinding out a 2-1 thriller over 100 Thieves on April 25 and a 2-0 rout of EG on May 1. Head-to-head history favors NRG slightly, but Sentinels' home-region momentum and roster synergy could swing map vetoes on Pearl or Lotus, while 100 Thieves' parallel result looms large for final seeding.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors no in-person meeting between President Trump and President Putin by June 30 at 93.6%, driven by the absence of any confirmed summit plans amid stalled Ukraine peace talks. Their most recent interaction was a 90-minute phone call on April 29 discussing a potential Victory Day truce, but Kremlin spokesmen reiterated in mid-April there are "no such plans at the moment" for a face-to-face. A floated U.S. invitation for Putin to the G20 in Miami drew noncommittal Kremlin responses, with Trump expressing doubts on attendance. Historical precedents, like the collapsed October 2025 Budapest proposal post-Alaska summit, underscore logistical, security, and geopolitical barriers. Shifts could arise from sudden Ukraine ceasefire progress, neutral-site diplomacy in Turkey or Switzerland, or escalated tensions prompting urgent bilateral talks.

markets.question: Will Algeria vs. Austria end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 27, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Algeria vs. Austria
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Saturday, June 27, 2026 between Algeria and Austria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Austria holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 43% implied probability for victory over Algeria (37.5%) in this pivotal 2026 FIFA World Cup Group J finale at neutral Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, with a draw at 29.5% underscoring the tight contest between evenly ranked sides (Austria 24th, Algeria 28th FIFA). Algeria's mounting injury crisis—goalkeepers Anthony Mandrea (shoulder surgery) and Melvin Mastil (groin hernia) sidelined, plus Yacine Titraoui (hamstring) and Amine Gouiri (muscle)—has eroded squad depth just weeks from kickoff, tempering their strong African qualifying campaign. Austria, buoyed by recent friendlies wins over Ghana (5-1) and South Korea (1-0) in late March, leverages superior recent form and defensive solidity despite their own concerns like Maximilian Wöber's muscular issues, keeping probabilities bunched in this high-stakes neutral-venue showdown absent recent head-to-head history.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal lead the Premier League table with 76 points from 35 games, two clear of Manchester City on 74, securing virtual locks on two Champions League spots entering matchday 36. Manchester United hold third at 65 points from 36 games, bolstered by consistent recent form, while Liverpool cling to fourth on 59 points, vulnerable to Aston Villa's surge—58 points from 35 games with a favorable run-in including Burnley. Key developments include Arsenal's resilience in the title race despite City's April win, and Villa's momentum threatening the top four. Remaining fixtures feature Arsenal at West Ham today, pivotal clashes like Liverpool versus Villa, and mid-table tests that could swing goal difference and qualification amid minor injury concerns for top squads.

eventMetadata.context_description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro holds a slim Polymarket edge over incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva at 41.1% to 40.5%, mirroring recent polls like AtlasIntel/Bloomberg (late April) and Ideia (early May) that show statistical ties or narrow Flávio leads in simulated second-round runoffs, with Lula holding a slight first-round advantage around 40% to Flávio's 36%. Lula's rising disapproval amid economic pressures and his age (79) erode incumbency benefits, while Flávio benefits from the Bolsonaro family's loyal base despite Jair's ongoing legal troubles. The right-wing vote fragments among Romeu Zema, Ronaldo Caiado, and others, keeping the first round competitive ahead of October 4. Separation could come from debates, economic releases, scandals, or endorsements before candidate registrations close.

markets.question: Will the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting be on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date (Pakistan Standard Time) on which the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran occurs.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

If no qualifying meeting occurs by May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM Pakistan Standard Time, this market will resolve to “No Meeting before May 11”.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices no US-Iran diplomatic meeting before May 11 at 99.5%, driven by the lack of any announced direct talks on May 10 amid a diplomatic stalemate. The US awaits Iran's response to a proposed one-page memorandum of understanding aimed at ending Strait of Hormuz hostilities and advancing nuclear negotiations, following an April ceasefire that persists despite recent US strikes on Iranian tankers and sporadic clashes. Indirect channels via Pakistan and China dominate, with no breakthroughs in the past week. A commanding lead reflects zero public scheduling signals; only late-breaking confirmation of impromptu representative-level diplomacy could challenge this before 11:59 PM ET resolution.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in San Francisco be between 50-51°F on May 10?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the San Francisco International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/san-francisco/KSFO.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 62-63°F, 64-65°F, and 66°F or higher at roughly 33% implied probabilities each for San Francisco International Airport (SFO), reflecting National Weather Service (NWS) forecasts projecting a high near 68°F today under decreasing clouds and west winds of 11-16 mph. This positioning arises from overnight model updates emphasizing persistent marine stratus—coastal low clouds typical of "May Gray"—which delayed morning clearing and kept early highs at 61°F from a 54°F start. Key differentiators include burn-off timing (earlier favors 66°F+ via greater solar insolation) versus prolonged overcast (62-63°F via reduced heating), plus onshore wind strength driving adiabatic cooling. Historical May averages near 64°F reinforce the tight race; NWS hourly observations through afternoon peak (2-4 PM) could tip the balance.

McLaren's recent Miami 2-3 finish highlights their race pace potential, elevating Norris to third in standings, while Red Bull's Max Verstappen sits seventh due to reliability woes and suboptimal car setup. The bunched top probabilities underscore a tight intra-Mercedes rivalry, looming upgrades, and 17 remaining races where strategy, pit stops, and DNF risks could reshape the drivers' title fight.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in forecast models for São Paulo's highest temperature on May 11, with implied probabilities tightly clustered at 37.5% for 20°C, 37.0% for 18°C, and 36.0% for 21°C, driven by an advancing cold front from the South. INMET's May 4-11 weekly outlook indicates mostly stable weather in the Southeast with low cloudiness and possible weak showers in southern São Paulo, but the front's timing could introduce southerly winds, increased cloud cover, and rain that suppress peak daytime heating below May climatological averages of 22-23°C. Model ensembles diverge on frontal passage speed—earlier arrival favors 18-19°C maxima via cooler air advection, while delays allow 20-21°C—pending tomorrow's updated INMET and CPTEC guidance. Urban heat island effects at official stations like Mirante de Santana add minor variability to observations.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $296 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Trader consensus favors Frederiksen at 64.5% implied probability, reflecting her party's lead and openness to centrist deals with Moderates (14 seats, leader Lars Løkke Rasmussen at 12.6%) or Venstre (18 seats), bolstered by post-election polls showing Social Democrats ahead. This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Gimhae Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Gimhae Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/busan/RKPK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Movimento Democrático Brasileiro (MDB) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.title: Next Brazil Senate Election: Most Seats Won
events.description:

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 83.5% implied probability to the Partido Liberal (PL) securing the most seats in Brazil's October 4 Senate election, where two-thirds of the 81 seats (54 total) face renewal across all states. This positioning stems from March-April state-level polls by AtlasIntel, Paraná Pesquisas, and others showing PL candidates leading or competitive in key races, including Distrito Federal (Michelle Bolsonaro), Rio de Janeiro (Claudio Castro), and Santa Catarina (Carlos Bolsonaro), projecting 15-20 PL wins amid the party's current largest Senate caucus of 16. Recent momentum from Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's statistical tie with President Lula in late April AtlasIntel and BTG Pactual/Nexus presidential surveys, coupled with the Senate's April 29 rejection of Lula's Supreme Court nominee, reinforces right-wing organizational strength over challengers like PODEMOS and UNIÃO Brasil, though candidate registration deadlines and national trends could influence final outcomes.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.
markets.groupItemTitle: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)

events.title: LoL: Anyone's Legend vs Weibo Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win against Weibo Gaming in the named LoL match in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win against Anyone's Legend in the LoL match.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend holds a commanding position in LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Ascend standings at 7-4 series record and third place, driving trader consensus with recent sweeps over Top Esports and Ninjas in Pyjamas plus a 2-1 BO3 win versus Weibo Gaming on April 25. Weibo Gaming sits seventh at 4-8, hampered by inconsistency following off-season roster losses like Breathe to Invictus Gaming and Erha to Team WE, with recent defeats to BLG and TES underscoring macro execution issues. In this double round-robin Fearless Draft matchup, AL's head-to-head edge (13-9 overall), stable lineup featuring Tarzan and Shanks, and top-four playoff implications heighten stakes amid current patch dynamics favoring aggressive laning.

markets.question: Valorant: Sentinels vs NRG (BO3) - VCT Americas Stage 1 Group Omega
markets.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Sentinels and NRG in the VCT Americas Stage 1 Group Omega, initially scheduled for May 10 at 8:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Sentinels" if Sentinels win the match against NRG.

This market will resolve to "NRG" if NRG win the match against Sentinels.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Group Omega of VCT Americas Stage 1, Sentinels and NRG sit at 2-2 records, with top-four advancement to playoffs on the line in this decisive Bo3 on May 10. Sentinels, featuring new duelist Jerrwin, rebounded from an 0-2 opener loss to KRÜ Esports with 2-0 sweeps over 100 Thieves and Evil Geniuses, but stumbled 1-2 to FURIA on May 2 amid close maps on Split, Breeze, and Haven. NRG mirrored the pattern, dropping 0-2 decisions to FURIA and KRÜ before grinding out a 2-1 thriller over 100 Thieves on April 25 and a 2-0 rout of EG on May 1. Head-to-head history favors NRG slightly, but Sentinels' home-region momentum and roster synergy could swing map vetoes on Pearl or Lotus, while 100 Thieves' parallel result looms large for final seeding.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid fragile ceasefire in the US-Iran conflict over the Strait of Hormuz, recent flare-ups including Qatari tanker incidents and US-revised UN Security Council resolutions demanding Iran halt attacks and mining have stalled progress toward a one-page memorandum of understanding proposed by Washington. Traders assess attendee lineups based on April Islamabad talks hosted by Pakistan, where US Vice President JD Vance, special envoy Steve Witkoff, and advisor Jared Kushner met Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. Araghchi's May 6 Beijing meeting with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi underscores multilateral diplomacy, with Qatar now mediating as both sides await Iran's response ahead of potential escalation or follow-up direct talks.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner commands 51% implied probability as the clear trader consensus favorite, anchored by his unassailable World No. 1 ATP ranking and dominant 2026 form, including a substantial points gap over rivals amid recent clay-court showings like Madrid and Rome. Carlos Alcaraz trails at 18% following withdrawals from Madrid Open and French Open due to injury concerns, dimming his grass prowess despite back-to-back Wimbledon triumphs. Novak Djokovic sits at 8.3%, buoyed by seven-time champion pedigree on the surface but tempered by age 39 and inconsistent results. Alexander Zverev's No. 3 ranking bolsters his 3.8% share, while grass hopefuls like Jack Draper gain traction pre-Halle and Queen's. Sinner skips Halle warm-up, eyeing direct Wimbledon prep as grass swing nears.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-05 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-05 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Rep. Scott Fitzgerald's strong reelection campaign in the solidly Republican WI-05 district, rated R+11 on the Cook Partisan Voting Index, drives trader consensus toward an 84% implied probability for a GOP hold. Fitzgerald, who won 64% in 2024, boasts over $1.4 million cash on hand as of late March, dwarfing Democratic challengers Andrew Beck and Ben Steinhoff's combined under $10,000. No polling exists yet, but forecasters like Cook Political Report rate it Solid Republican. Absent major shifts before the June 1 filing deadline or August 11 primaries, the district's conservative suburban base around Waukesha sustains the lopsided odds, with no notable developments in the past 30 days.

markets.question: Will Bruno Mars have the greatest number of monthly Spotify listeners this month?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed artist with the greatest number of monthly listeners according to Spotify on May 31, 2026, 12PM ET.

The monthly listener count is listed on each artist's public Spotify profile. Only primary artist profiles will qualify; features or collaborations under another artist profile will not count towards the featured artist's total.

In the event of an exact tie for the number of monthly listeners, this market will resolve in favor of the listed artist whose name comes first in alphabetical order.

If Spotify is down at the listed time on the listed date, this market will resolve based on the most recent available data.

The resolution source for this market will be Spotify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Bruno Mars as the May Spotify top artist frontrunner at 42% implied probability, edging Justin Bieber's 31% amid a razor-close monthly listeners race—Bieber at 144 million versus Bruno's 137 million as of May 9. Bieber's explosive post-Coachella 2026 headlining surge, delivering his biggest streaming day ever (105 million on April 20) and a 1,800% weekly jump to 431 million, propelled him past Bruno early May. However, Bruno's sustained momentum from his 2026 album The Romantic, chart-dominating single "I Just Might," and billion-stream collabs like "Die With A Smile" (Lady Gaga) and "APT." (ROSÉ) keeps traders bullish on his trajectory. Drake lags at 8.5% on consistent hip-hop streams without fresh releases; remaining May streaming data and potential drops will decide this volatile contest.

markets.question: Will Xi meet with Takaichi by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Sanae Takaichi meets with Xi Jinping between market creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Takaichi and Jinping are present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's hawkish stance, including late 2025 remarks on potential military involvement in a Taiwan contingency, continues to strain China-Japan relations six months later, with Beijing maintaining a hardline posture and rejecting Tokyo's recent overtures for a summit at the 2026 APEC gathering. No in-person meeting between Takaichi and President Xi Jinping has occurred since their October 2025 APEC sideline talks, amid ongoing disputes over Senkaku/Diaoyu islands and rare earth exports. Takaichi's February 2026 election victory bolstered her security agenda, prompting alliances with Australia and Vietnam, while trader consensus reflects uncertainty over bilateral diplomacy before year-end, potentially catalyzed by multilateral forums like G20 or APEC.

markets.question: Will Golden Lions win LRS 2026 Split 1?
